observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Executive Officer the following:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count efficiently and without delay following the PIP, and submit the required reports such as the RPCPPE, ICF, List of PPEs found at the Station, and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs within the designated period; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the recorded asset balances with the physical counts, promptly investigate any discrepancies and ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan and initiate the formal transfer of these properties to the Department of Education or their respective end users, in accordance with applicable laws and guidelines; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and record the necessary adjusting journal entries to derecognize the subject assets from the Municipality’s books upon transfer to the appropriate agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to coordinate in identifying completed infrastructure projects for reclassification to the appropriate PPE accounts and to ensure that all ongoing projects are properly recorded under the Construction in Progress (CIP) account based on validated progress reports;
Require the Municipal Engineer to regularly submit updated reports on completed and ongoing infrastructure projects, along with supporting documents such as certificates of completion, project inspection reports, and percentage of completion reports; and
Require the Municipal Engineer to maintain a CIP Ledger Card for all infrastructure assets to support accurate monitoring, reporting, and reconciliation with the accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the General Services Office (GSO) to secure the necessary supporting documents from the donating agencies, such as Deeds of Donation, Memoranda of Agreement, and any related transmittal or acceptance documents. The GSO should also coordinate with relevant agencies to establish the fair value of the donated equipment, which will serve as the basis for proper recording;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the immediate recognition and recording of the donated transportation equipment in the LGU’s books of accounts at their fair value, in accordance with the Government Accounting Manual; and
Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to strengthen internal control measures to ensure that all future donations are adequately documented, properly valued, and promptly recorded upon receipt by the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and MEEDO to regularly submit a report on the collections and uncollected rentals including a Certified List of Stallholders, to the Municipal Accountant as a basis for recording the transactions and facilitate the collection of unpaid rentals;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the total unpaid rentals in the books of the Municipality to the appropriate receivable and income account based on the Certified List of Stallholders with the duly approved Contract of Lease and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each stallholder; and
Strictly enforce lease agreements and the relevant ordinances by requiring all lessees to sign updated lease agreements with clear terms on rental payments, penalties for late payments, and consequences of non-compliance.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish copy of the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due to the Accounting Office and ensure that the amounts of delinquent taxes are incorporated in the established receivable amount to be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant; and
Municipal Accountant to recognize the correct amount of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts and reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer’s records.

The Municipal Accountant to affect the necessary adjustments and refrain from incurring expense  accounts in the Trust Fund books, ensuring that the Trust Fund be used solely to record the receipt and disbursement of funds held in trust and intended for a specific purpose.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Compute for the depreciation expense for those PPE not subjected to depreciation in compliance with IPSAS 17; and
Communicate with the ECPAC Office regarding the generation of a lapsing schedule.

We have recommended the following courses of actions:
Strictly follow the perpetual inventory method in recording and accounting of inventories for distribution.
Require physical count of supplies and materials at least once a year in compliance with Section 194 of the GAM for LGU’s, Volume I. Conduct physical count of unused inventories, if any, for the recognition of inventories accounts reported without balances in the books. Submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories thereafter.
Prepare and maintain the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports in relation to receipt and issuance of inventories for distribution.
Take up the necessary adjusting entries for the incorrect use of accounts. Use the appropriate accounts provided under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to furnish the Accounting office with a copy of Schedule of Earned Leave Credits with money value for all LGU personnel as basis by the Accounting Office to record the leave accruals and in the meantime that budget is unavailable, it is advised that this information be disclosed as unrecorded liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Cashier to liquidate her cash advances such as salaries and wages, allowances, honoraria and other similar payments to officials and employees and other payees within five days after each 15 day/end of the month pay period in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting of cash advance to the disbursing officer unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
Advise the recipients or payees with regard to the period they can claim.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to –
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact and daily or at the frequency prescribed by the regulation.
Keep track of the checks received from payor to prevent delays of deposit and send demand letter immediately in the event of insufficiency.
Give priority to the Acting Municipal Treasurer’s use of the vehicle in order to comply with the regulation.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive  –
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly or at least once in every quarter to establish the correct balance of accountability of the AO at the earliest time possible. Any account found unrecorded can be easily traced and the necessary corrections/recording can be effected immediately.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entry to correct the negative balance of the Cash – Local Treasury accounts immediately.
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required cashbook to record his cash advances and liquidations regardless of frequency of transaction.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) for the Collecting Officers.
Require the Collecting Officers to prepare monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) while the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and properly accomplish the Consolidated Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and submit to COA in the prescribed form provided in the GAM Manual not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.
Include the inventory of checks being maintained and issued in the preparation of the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms in order to prepare an accurate and complete report.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive directs the Cashier/Accountable Officer to:
Record daily transactions in the cashbooks prescribed in Section 32 of the GAM, Vol. II in the order they occur including specifying the date of liquidations. Refrain from recording liquidation when no liquidation is actually made;
Balance, rule and close at the end of the day her cashbook and foot and close the books and certify the balances at the end of each month; and
Reconcile her cashbook with the accounting records regularly or at least once in every quarter to establish the correct balance of accountability of the AO at the earliest time possible.
Use and submit Report of Cash Disbursements (sample attached hereto) in the liquidation of her cash advances instead of Report of Disbursements together with the liquidation documents pertaining thereto. For facilitative purposes, keep a copy of the report in the book-bound liquidation submitted for audit.

We have recommended and management agreed to –
Do not allow payment of cash advances without complete documentary requirements set forth under COA Circular 2012-001. Submit pertinent DV’s to COA always with the prescribed supporting documents to avoid suspension thereof.
Submit immediately legal basis for all cash advances granted for allowances and other similar expenses other than salaries during the period under audit.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive directs the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 updated as of March 31, 2021, on the modes of procurement, and to refrain from procurement of goods thru reimbursement to obtain prices advantageous to the government. observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to –
Submit bank reconciliation statements within the prescribed period.
Enroll the bank accounts in the lbpiaccess website to download the bank statements to ensure timely preparation and submission of the bank reconciliation statement.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Require the Accountant to prioritize the retrieval of journal entry vouchers from prior years to serve as a basis for the preparation of adjusting entry.
Instruct the Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to undertake reconciliation of records.
Stop the granting of cash advance to official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
Direct the AO liquidate his regular cash advances, such as salaries and wages; allowances; honoraria and other similar payments to officials and employees, within five days after each 15 day/end of the month pay period in accordance with COA circular No. 97-002.

We reiterate the following recommendations and management agreed that -
The Local Chief Executive to create inventory committee; to prepare a timetable for the conduct of Physical Inventory including Local Road Network and coordinate with the Inventory Committee members for a cohesive planning.
The GSO to initiate the disposal in accordance with the requirements provided in COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 79 of PD No.;
The Accounting unit to disclose in their notes under Property, Plant and Equipment, the estimated amount of all the unserviceable motor vehicles subject for disposition; and
The Accounting unit and GSO to maintain complete property ledger cards for all PPE owned by the agency.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Direct the BAC members to refund the amount representing excess payments of honoraria.
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit all lacking documents and ensure that prospective disbursement relative to BAC honorarium are compliant with the supporting documents listed in COA Circular 2012-001 to support the validity and propriety of claim.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Direct the Liquidating Officer to deposit collections intact and daily to the depository bank or as soon as the collections reach P10,000.00.
Instruct the Liquidating Officer to stop using the collections for encashment of any checks of the LGU as this will only cause delay in its deposit to the bank.
Require the liquidating officer and the Cashier to make use of the ETRACS in every remittance and deposit of collections. Also, include the Summary of RCD generated in the system upon submission of the documents to the accounting office.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Require the accountable officer to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover her maximum cash accountability.
Management should determine the maximum cash accountability to be assigned to the accountable officer before the application with the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Require the BAC Secretary –

to submit to COA the approved contracts together with its supporting papers for the said perfected projects for COA’s review.

to submit prospective contracts within 5 days upon its perfection in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.
Advise management headed by the Local Chief Executive to revisit the internal control policies over LGU procurement operations to identify and address these recurring lapses over submission of contracts.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Direct the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretary to require all Department Heads to include in their PPMP items necessary in their respective programs/activities.
Require all members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the Head of the Procuring Entity to attend seminars/trainings that will improve their knowledge in the procurement.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those of the third parties involved in the execution of the government projects.
We further recommended that future procurements be deducted with the 10% retention money or require a special bank guarantee which is equivalent to 10% of the total contract price, otherwise, expenses due to the defects of the goods or project within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials approving the payment.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the above-mentioned lacking documents and to ensure that prospective payments are supported with complete documents to establish the propriety of the transactions.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the projects and activities to be funded and a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular 2012-002 and require the LDRRMO to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Direct the Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board to initiate a written representation to follow up the approval of the agency’s Solid Waste Management Plan.
Direct management to adopt measures for the implementation of segregation of waste under all its jurisdiction.
Direct the Local Chief Executive to increase the number of MRF pursuant to Section 33 of RA 9003 in order to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Ensure that SEF funds are utilized accordingly as set forth in the annual investment plan in order not to hamper the implementation of activities and projects.
Ensure that expenditures charged to SEF are in accordance with the School Improvement Plan (SIP) and the Division Education Development are formulated collaboratively with stakeholders.
Direct the Local School Board to prepare and submit Annual SEF Budget with the prescribed form and timeline pursuant to the new Joint Circular Dep Ed-DBM and DILG No. 1 s. 2017.

We recommended and management agreed to –
The Local Chief Executive to ensure timely implementation of all 20% development projects.
The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the projects and at the same time respond to the causes of delays on the actual implementation;
Revisit guidelines in the preparation of budget for 20% Local Government Development Fund to ensure responsive estimate of project cost.
Provide specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs identified in the AIP and target schedule for each procurement activities in the APP.

We recommended that the management team or local finance committee prepare the Annual Investment Program (AIP) in accordance with the provisions of DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s.2007 dated March 8, 2007.  It is also emphasized that expected outputs or results for all PPAs must be in quantified terms in order to set criteria for reconciling actual outputs, duly prepared and signed by the Local Development and Planning Officer and Budget Officer and attested by the Local Chief Executive or his duly authorized representative.
observations.managementAction: Management assures that the AIP for the current year is now in line with the regulation.
Management requested for a staggered salary deduction.
